reason: Aaron, a Hebrew leader (the high priest), explicitly blames the people for the golden calf incident. When Moses confronts him, Aaron responds by saying 'You know the people, that they are prone to evil' (כִּ֥י בְרָ֖ע הֽוּא) and then recounts how the people demanded he make a god for them, presenting himself as having been coerced by their pressure rather than accepting responsibility for his own role in the sin.
